# rasuvaeff/rector-named-literals

Rector rule `AddNameToLiteralArgumentRector` — adds parameter names to literal
arguments (the "boolean trap" fix). Namespace `Rasuvaeff\RectorNamedLiterals`.

## Install

```bash
composer require --dev rasuvaeff/rector-named-literals
```

## Usage

```php
// rector.php — bool literals only (default)
->withRules([AddNameToLiteralArgumentRector::class])

// opt-in numeric/string literals
->withConfiguredRule(AddNameToLiteralArgumentRector::class, [
    AddNameToLiteralArgumentRector::BOOL => true,
    AddNameToLiteralArgumentRector::NUMERIC => true,  // 3, 2.5, -5
    AddNameToLiteralArgumentRector::STRING => true,   // 'text'
])
```

## Rules

- `send($m, true, false)` → `send($m, urgent: true, queue: false)`.
- Matched literal not last → every FOLLOWING positional arg is named too
  (PHP forbids positional after named).
- Skips (call untouched): interface-declared callees (implementations may
  rename params), `@no-named-arguments` on function/class/ancestors, argument
  unpacking, variadic target params, unresolvable callees, first-class
  callables, built-in callees whose planned names fail native reflection
  (PHPStan's signature map invents `min(arg1, arg2, …)` variants for variadic
  built-ins — PHP rejects those names at runtime).
- `null` literals: use Rector's own AddNameToNullArgumentRector alongside.
- Unknown config key / non-bool value → the rector run fails.
- Caveat: a named argument makes the parameter name part of your contract with
  the callee — apply to vendor calls consciously.
